findings.evidence: Proof body uses only `Finset.prod_image`, `Finset.mul_prod_erase`, `Finset.prod_pos`, `mul_neg_of_neg_of_pos`, `not_isSquare_of_neg`; `not_isSquare_of_neg` (mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Order/Ring/Basic.lean:32) and `Finset.prod_pos` (Algebra/Order/BigOperators/GroupWithZero/Finset.lean:107) need only Semiring/MonoidWithZero + order, not a field.
findings.fix: Weaken to `[CommRing K] [LinearOrder K] [IsStrictOrderedRing K]` (linear ordered comm ring). The sole consumer is `K = ℚ`, so the `finrank_adjoin_I_sqrt_primes` corollary is unaffected, and the combinator becomes reusable for integer/ring radicands.
findings.issue: `not_isSquare_prod_optionNeg` requires `[Field K]`, but the proof never uses inverses or any field-specific fact; all steps (Finset.prod_pos, mul_neg_of_neg_of_pos, not_isSquare_of_neg, finite products of IsSquare) hold over an ordered commutative ring. Field is unused / stronger than the natural Mathlib level for this square-class lemma.

summary: The `not_isSquare_prod_optionNeg` combinator carries an unused `[Field K]` assumption: its proof uses only ordered-comm-ring facts (Finset products, not_isSquare_of_neg, mul_neg_of_neg_of_pos, Finset.prod_pos), no inverses. Stating it over a linearly ordered commutative ring is the natural level and makes the 'reusable core' usable for non-field radicands (e.g. ℤ).
